rail.name: Rail On Site (ROS)
rail.status_label: Interface driven - commitment and access control required

progress.basis: Railways Management progress (construction progress from scope page)

milestones.name: North Railway - Section 7 ready for commissioning

focus: Keep terrace filling 4B-5 and rail access/interface commitments moving through W24-W30.
Close groundworks commitment so railway crossings can start without further interface slippage.
Track Rail Memo 20/21 and hydrogen-pipeline crossing design inputs as engineering gates.
Expedite GRK rail ITP resubmission and keep quality documentation ahead of field work.
blockers: No groundworks commitment: works cannot start with railway crossings until IDOM/Stegra alignment is ready.
Scrap Yard micropiling design is complete, but construction execution remains pending.
Existing power supply clash at Rail Section 5 can restrict rail groundworks until a technical solution is confirmed.
quality: Close follow-up for GRK ITP resubmission for Rail, Roads and Ponds.
GRK Rail ITPs require resubmission / approval and closure of comments before workfront acceleration.
Monitor rail-related QOCs/NCRs, inspection records and Omega submissions weekly.
A total of 10 test packs remain pending for submission and documentation; Rail ITP documentation is part of the current quality gate.
rail.insight: Rail On Site (ROS) is controlled by construction access, design-interface and quality-documentation gates. The critical PM attention is to keep terrace filling and rail engineering memos synchronized while closing GRK ITP comments and open interface commitments.
engineering_items: W24 weekly report: Rail Memo 20 expected on 06 Jul including all GW definition; Memo 21 will finalize rail design after summer break.
Hydrogen pipeline crossing and existing power supply at Section 5 remain design/interface gates for rail groundworks.
Only the remaining rail engineering gates should be tracked as actions; do not reuse global BOP engineering progress for Rail.
rail.engineering_note: Rail-specific value available from ROS May 2026 presentation: 99% IFC+IFR.
rail.next_week_fc: 2026 W25: Terrace filling 4B-5 and rail access/interface controls.

roads.name: Roads
roads.status_label: Early progress - backfill sequence to protect dates

progress.basis: Roads & Ditches Management progress (construction progress from scope page)

focus: Complete backfilling Avenue 1 connection to municipality road.
Issue CR / approval path to start Avenue 9.5 backfilling and Road 6.0 works.
Track Road 6 / Road 6.1 readiness against sub-base start commitments.
Expedite GRK Road ITP resubmission and documentation approval before additional road works.
blockers: Road 6.0 CR must be reviewed and approved to allow GRK use of Road 6.0 as laydown area for Road 6.1 / 6.2.
Road 5.1 / 5.2 has design holds due to missing TOPs to NPB207 and unresolved building definition.
Boden Energy switch houses conflict with the East ditch/service road and require Stegra decision.
quality: Close follow-up for GRK ITP resubmission for Rail, Roads and Ponds.
A total of 10 test packs remain pending for submission and documentation; Road ITP documentation is part of the current quality gate.
GRK Roads ITPs require resubmission / approval and alignment with Stegra/EPCM comments.
Backfilling inspection records must be complete before covering works.
Monitor NOIs, QOCs, NCRs, HDPE welding documentation and material traceability for road-related works.
roads.insight: Roads are in an early physical-progress phase and are highly dependent on backfilling approvals and quality inspection readiness. The control focus is to remove CR / ITP / inspection blockers before Road 6, Road 6.1 and Avenue 9.5 works compress the sequence.
engineering_items: Prio 5 / Sweco SUB33 area delivery dates for Roads & Ditches remain a design gate; IFR postponed and IFC follows after the IFR package.
Road 6.0 / Road 6.1 execution requires CR / field-change or site-instruction process alignment before construction sequencing is locked.
Road 5.1 / 5.2 and service-road design are affected by TOP / building definition and Boden Energy switch-house decisions.
roads.engineering_note: No scope-specific Roads engineering percent published; controlled through design/RFI/CR gates.
roads.next_week_fc: 2026 W25: Completion backfilling Avenue 1 connection with municipality road.

ponds.name: Ponds
ponds.status_label: Quality and commitment watch - GRK documentation is a gate

progress.basis: Ponds Management progress (construction progress from scope page)

focus: Continue West Pond 1 geomembrane installation and prepare material cover activities.
Start East Stormwater Pond and Contact Water Pond construction in the committed W25-W33 window.
Resolve Contact Water Pond commitment / pricing and remaining West Pond 3 works.
Expedite GRK Ponds ITP, HDPE welding, traceability and inspection records.
Maintain excavation, water-management and environmental controls around active pond works.
blockers: Commitment not in place for Contact Water Pond; IDOM, GRK and Stegra must expedite the process.
Remaining works for West Pond 3 completion require expedited alignment.
GRK started Ponds works before approved ITPs in prior week; inspection records and ITP closure must be controlled.
quality: Close follow-up for GRK ITP resubmission for Rail, Roads and Ponds.
A total of 10 test packs remain pending for submission and documentation; Pond ITP documentation is part of the current quality gate.
GRK Ponds ITPs were rejected / require resubmission and approval before the next execution step.
Retrospective inspection records are required for works started before approved ITPs.
HDPE welding, membrane installation, traceability and test-pack records must be controlled weekly.
ponds.insight: Ponds control is no longer only construction progress. The main exposure is the combination of GRK documentation maturity, ITP approval, HDPE / geomembrane quality controls, and commitments for Contact Water Pond and West Pond 3 completion.
engineering_items: Contact Water Pond membrane solution remains an engineering/design gate: HDPE + GCL / protective geotextile alternative under follow-up.
East Stormwater Pond and Contact Water Pond require design/quality readiness before the W25-W33 construction window is protected.
West Pond works require alignment between design, ITP approval, HDPE/geomembrane documentation and inspection records.
ponds.engineering_note: No scope-specific Ponds engineering percent published; controlled through membrane/RFI/ITP design gates.
ponds.next_week_fc: 2026 W25: West Pond 1 geomembrane installation; start construction of East Stormwater Pond.
